title: Make the simulation deterministic
description: Remove non-determinism (variable dt, float order, unseeded RNG, wall-clock) so the same inputs reproduce the same state for replay and netcode.
body: Steps: 1. Fix the timestep so steps are identical across machines and runs. 2. Seed all RNG explicitly and advance it deterministically. 3. Make iteration order stable (sorted/array-backed, not hash-iteration order). 4. Avoid wall-clock time, uninitialized memory, and platform-divergent float behavior in the sim. 5. Validate by replaying recorded inputs and comparing a state checksum each tick. Rollback point: before changing the float/RNG model (affects existing saves/replays). Validation: replaying the same input log yields identical per-tick checksums on the same and other machines; any checksum divergence pinpoints a remaining non-deterministic source. This is the prerequisite for lockstep netcode.
